title: Ctenanthe steudneri
intro: Ctenanthe steudneri, commonly known as the Prayer Plant, is a tropical plant species belonging to the Marantaceae family, characterized by its beautiful, uniquely patterned leaves. The leaves of Ctenanthe steudneri have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Ctenanthe steudneri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ctenanthe steudneri is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including coughs, colds, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Ctenanthe steudneri is a species of flowering plant in the family Marantaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of South America. The plant is also known as the Grey Prayer Plant due to its beautiful foliage. Ctenanthe steudneri is often classified as a type of evergreen perennial plant. It belongs to the genus Ctenanthe, which is a group of flowering plants within the Marantaceae family.

side_effects: Ctenanthe steudneri, also known as the Never Never Plant, is a low-maintenance houseplant that is generally considered non-toxic.
